note: CURATED public swimming / river-access spots on the Lower American River and at the Sacramento River confluence. Coordinates sit on the water: spots that coincide with a state monitoring station use that station's on-river coordinate; the rest are geocoded (OSM Nominatim) and projected onto the OSM American River centerline. NOT the Strava heatmap (proprietary/auth-gated) and NOT from the monitoring datasets. 'intensity' popularity weights (0-1) are approximate. Popularity does NOT imply safety: several popular spots are also dangerous.
sources: Sacramento County Regional Parks — American River Parkway

spots.name: Tiscornia Beach (Confluence)
spots.note: Sandy family swim beach where the American meets the Sacramento River. Life jackets on site, no lifeguard. Beware the sudden underwater ledge just offshore.

spots.name: Discovery Park
spots.note: Large park at the confluence; waist-deep wading areas popular with families.

spots.name: Sutter's Landing
spots.note: River access and beach near downtown; swimming, kayaking and canoeing.

spots.name: Paradise Beach (Glenn Hall Park)
spots.note: Oxbow cove near Sac State (Carlson & Sandburg). Very popular with families and students.

spots.name: Howe Avenue Access
spots.note: River access along the parkway near the Howe Ave / Cal Expo reach.

spots.name: Watt Avenue Access
spots.note: River access at the Watt Ave bridge.

spots.name: River Bend Park
spots.note: Formerly Goethe Park (Rancho Cordova). Beaches plus quiet channels with deeper holes off the equestrian trail.

spots.name: Rossmoor Bar
spots.note: Gravel-bar river access in Rancho Cordova.

spots.name: Ancil Hoffman Park
spots.note: Large Carmichael park on a river bend; popular beaches.

spots.name: Sunrise Recreation Area
spots.note: Lower & Upper Sunrise — sandy swim beaches with parking; one of the most popular swimming areas on the Lower American.

spots.name: Sailor Bar
spots.note: Fair Oaks river access and beach; rafting put-in.

spots.name: Hagan Community Park
spots.note: Pebbled shoreline with small sandy stretches for wading.

spots.name: Clay Banks
spots.note: Popular cliff/swim spot — but ALSO a known drowning hazard: steep drop-offs and swift, cold current.

spots.name: Camp Pollock
spots.note: Riverside access near the Northgate / Del Paso area.

spots.name: Nimbus Flat (Lake Natoma)
spots.note: Calm Lake Natoma swimming/aquatic area at Nimbus Flat (Folsom end), above the Lower American River.
